title: Luffy Stands Up to the Bandits
description: On pages 31–34, this part follows the Romance Dawn flashback and focuses on how Luffy’s pirate dream is formed through Shanks, Higuma, and the straw hat promise.
scene: Luffy’s loyalty to Shanks pushes him into danger as the bandits turn their attention toward him.

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: I was just drinking and talking to my men.

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: I didn't say anything to offend you.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: WHYD HE HAVE TO PICK A FIGHT WITH THOSE GUYS?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I don't know what Luffy did...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: AND I HAVE NO INTENTION OF FIGHTING YOU PEOPLE...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: But if it's money you want, you'll get it.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You know how the world works.

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: But I'm afraid it's too late.

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: NO ONE CAN SAVE THIS BRAT NOW.

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: HE'S DONE THE UNPARDON- ABLE... HE ATTACKED ME...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: CAN'T TAKE THAT FROM THIS LITTLE RUBBER- SPINED FREAK!

dialogue.speaker: Makino
dialogue.text: THAT DOES IT. I'm not gonna sell you.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Nobody came to greet us at the Harbor.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: WAS WONDER- ING WHAT WAS GOING ON...

dialogue.speaker: Higuma
dialogue.text: OH, IT'S YOU MOUNTAIN BANDITS AGAIN!

summary: Luffy Stands Up to the Bandits is the main story beat of this section. The dialogue and reactions show how the chapter’s conflict develops, while the scene keeps the focus on the characters’ choices rather than technical OCR structure.
